name: The Death Strip
description: The forty-meter no-man's-land between the eastern and western barriers — raked gravel, floodlit from both sides, watched from the watchtower above. Tonight it is empty of people but full of the tension of its own history: the gravel has been raked smooth today, as it is every day, by guards who are now standing at the gate wondering if they will be the last men to do it.
atmosphericDetails: The raked gravel of the death strip, perfectly smooth under the floodlights — a surface designed to show footprints, showing none tonight
The watchtower above the strip, its observation window dark — the guard inside has not moved in forty minutes
The sound of the crowd on both sides of the strip, creating a stereo effect that is disorienting and immersive simultaneously
